items.q: How do you say 'I am going to eat' in Spanish (near future)?
items.why: Students forget the 'a'. The structure is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is essential and links the conjugated ir to the action. Voy comer is ungrammatical.

items.q: Which Spanish 'you' do you use with a stranger or elderly person?
items.why: Students use tú with everyone like English 'you'. Spanish distinguishes FORMAL (usted) for politeness/distance and INFORMAL (tú) for friends/family. Usted takes third-person verb forms, which trips students up.

items.q: How do you say 'I am watching TV right now' emphasising the action is ongoing?
items.correct: Estoy viendo la televisión ahora mismo.
items.why: Students use simple present. For ONGOING actions in progress, use ESTAR + GERUNDIO (-ando/-iendo): estoy comiendo, estoy estudiando, estoy hablando. Note: estar, not ser. Useful for describing current activities in tech/free time topics.

items.q: Who wrote La casa de los espiritus?
items.why: It's Isabel Allende. “Mario Vargas Llosa” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Argentina. “Chile” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Vargas Llosa win the Nobel?
items.why: The real figure is 2010. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
